page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs with 1 thread per core, and is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations reveal moderate single-core capabilities alongside constrained multi-core and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU test scored 1880.19, placing it in the average tier, while the multi-core score of 1926.99 is categorized as poor. Memory bandwidth tests show 104.68 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drop to 7.6 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform moderately, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 27.58 GB/sec, though complex database operations and Redis SET operations reflect lower-tier performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a dedicated hardware profile suitable for entry-level workloads that require stable CPU allocation without the variability of burstable instances. Given its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of local storage, it is appropriate for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, small-scale single-threaded processing, and development or testing environments where high memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling are not critical.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.large is a general-purpose virtual machine utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8369B processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1880.19 and single-threaded gzip compression of 27.58 GB/sec, though multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth remain limited. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without burstable performance degradation. It is appropriate for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic single-threaded applications.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with balanced memory allocation for entry-level workloads and stable single-threaded performance.
